gold_claim: Speculative decoding works because verification can score many drafted tokens in one target-model forward pass.
supporting_text: Verification means doing one forward pass over these generated tokens to see how likely it is that the big model would have generated them.
why_valuable: Identifies the architectural asymmetry that makes speculative decoding possible.

gold_claim: Effective diffusion MPC requires both an accurate dynamics model and a planner powerful enough to select action sequences.
supporting_text: One the dynamics model need to be accurate to avoid the problem of compounding errors and uh two the planning algorithm also needs to be powerful enough to select a good sequence of actions.
why_valuable: States the two practical bottlenecks for model-based control.

gold_claim: Observation-only diffusion learning can exploit video-only data, addressing robotics data scarcity.
supporting_text: The main benefit of this is it allows you to leverage uh uh video only data to learn from video only data because for robotics uh the data is a many bottleneck.
why_valuable: Connects a learning formulation to the central robotics data bottleneck.

gold_claim: A JEPA-style world model predicts the next latent embedding conditioned on an action instead of generating pixels directly.
supporting_text: you're going to train an action condition forecasting module this predictor to be able to predict what is the next latent embedding going to look like when I execute this action.
why_valuable: Captures the move toward latent predictive world models.

gold_claim: Power-law asymptotes can estimate the best achievable loss under effectively infinite compute.
supporting_text: And this is in some sense a quantification of your best possible performance under infinite compute.
why_valuable: Defines a new optimization target for data-constrained pretraining.

gold_claim: The main bottleneck in vanilla speculative decoding is the sequential dependency between drafting and verification.
supporting_text: a big bottleneck in vanilla speculative decoding is the sequential dependence between the small llama and the big llama.
why_valuable: Reframes the performance limit as dependency structure rather than draft-model speed alone.

gold_claim: The next speculative draft round can begin before current verification completes by predicting likely verification outcomes.
supporting_text: we are going to start drafting the next round on top of those immediately while verification is taking place.
why_valuable: Describes the core concurrency mechanism behind SSD.

gold_claim: A latent world-model method claims to replace multiple anti-collapse tricks with a substantially simpler regularization approach.
supporting_text: instead of having to use some manner of trick or like special method or a bunch of like hyperparameter tuning schedule, we're instead going to really drastically simplify this and go for a more elegant method.
why_valuable: Challenges the assumption that world-model collapse requires complex engineering tricks.

gold_claim: Overparameterization may remain explainable by classical generalization theory because flatter minima are more compressible.
supporting_text: flat minima are known to be more compressible than sharp minima and so overparameterization fits within existing theories
why_valuable: Pushes against the claim that deep-learning generalization is fundamentally mysterious.
reviewer_note: Legacy non-risk label moved from expected_risks to epistemic_status: 未必

change_history.note: Legacy non-risk label moved from expected_risks to epistemic_status: 未必

gold_claim: When data constrained, an ensemble of small models can outperform one equally sized large model.
supporting_text: it's better to train an ensemble of a bunch of small models when you're data constrained than to train one really large model.
why_valuable: Suggests a different scaling axis from simply increasing model size.

gold_claim: Speculative verification-outcome prediction becomes less reliable as batch size increases.
supporting_text: as batch size increases, you're going to fail to predict some of the sequences verification outcomes.
why_valuable: Identifies a scaling failure mode and cache-miss trade-off.

gold_claim: MPC fails in practice if dynamics errors compound or the planner cannot find a strong action sequence.
supporting_text: the dynamics model need to be accurate to avoid the problem of compounding errors and uh two the planning algorithm also needs to be powerful enough
why_valuable: Separates modeling and planning failure sources.

gold_claim: Multi-step dynamics modeling is intended to extend rollout horizons without accumulating as much error.
supporting_text: a multi-step um uh dynamics model which uh allows you to uh evolve for a long time horizon without a lot of compounding error.
why_valuable: States why multi-step modeling matters beyond architectural novelty.

change_history.note: Reviewer confirmed the prior result is unchanged; legacy pending default adapted to approve.

gold_claim: A world-model method performs poorly on a low-dimensional two-room environment because its representation objective favors high-dimensional embeddings.
supporting_text: we don't do so well on this but that's because we're promoting like really high dimensional healthy embeddings and it's a very low dimensional problem.
why_valuable: Shows a concrete mismatch between representation assumptions and task dimensionality.

gold_claim: Under the standard data-constrained recipe, spending more compute on larger overparameterized models eventually increases overfitting and loss.
supporting_text: even if you are willing to spend more compute, as you train more and more overparameterized models, you start to overfit more quickly and your loss starts to increase after a certain point.
why_valuable: Shows why compute-optimal scaling recipes fail in a data-limited regime.

gold_claim: A reported $1.03 billion raise is presented as evidence of the scale of investment in world-model training.
supporting_text: Yan Lakun's raise of $1.03 billion dollars back in March basically just to train world models
why_valuable: A large capital signal may indicate industrial commitment to the route.
verification_requirement: verify person, company, amount and date externally

gold_claim: The latent-space world-model method is claimed to be roughly 50 times faster than competitors.
supporting_text: it's about 50 times faster than any of the competition across the board because it's doing all this work in the latent space
why_valuable: Provides a potentially route-changing efficiency claim.

gold_claim: The model is reported to run on one GPU with under 24 GB VRAM and only 15 million parameters.
supporting_text: you can actually boot this thing up on like a single card, less than 24 gigabytes of VRAM and it's only 15 million parameters.
why_valuable: Establishes deployment and replication requirements.

gold_claim: Human-generated internet text is projected to grow about 3% annually while pretraining compute grows roughly four- to fivefold annually.
supporting_text: human generated text on the internet grows by roughly 3% per year. And the amount of compute that we're spending on pre-training is growing by roughly four or 5x per year.
why_valuable: Quantifies why future training becomes increasingly data constrained.

change_history.note: Reviewer confirmed the prior result is unchanged; legacy pending default adapted to approve.

gold_claim: A continued-pretraining setup claims to match 73 billion tokens of training data using 4 billion tokens, a roughly 17x data-efficiency gain.
supporting_text: you can match the performance of training on the full 73 billion tokens even using only 4 billion tokens which is roughly a 17x data efficiency win.
why_valuable: A large data-efficiency claim could materially change continued-pretraining strategy.
verification_requirement: verify CPT setup and all figures

gold_claim: At high usage, inference cost can dominate training cost, and RL training compute may exceed pretraining compute.
supporting_text: inference costs are dominating training costs, but even within training, RL is starting to exceed the compute requirements of pre-training.
why_valuable: Challenges planning that treats pretraining as the dominant lifetime cost.

gold_claim: Explicit world-model policies require a separate mechanism to propose candidate actions for evaluation.
supporting_text: you actually need to propose action candidates to evaluate with the world model
why_valuable: Surfaces an extra search/proposal cost hidden by high-level world-model descriptions.

gold_claim: Data-constraint theory predicts a finite asymptotic loss for an optimally regularized model under infinite compute.
supporting_text: the scaling law has an asmtote which is 3.43 in this case. And this characterizes the performance of the best possible regularized model in this setting if you had like infinite compute.
why_valuable: Makes the limiting performance of a recipe measurable rather than qualitative.
verification_requirement: verify asymptote spelling, value and loss definition

gold_claim: Distillation can retain much of the data-efficiency gain while reducing the inference-compute cost of ensembles.
supporting_text: you can take an eight ensemble which is roughly 2.4 billion total parameters and you can distill it into a single dense 300 million parameter model ... while retaining roughly 83% of the loss improvement.
why_valuable: Addresses the deployment objection to compute-heavy data-efficient recipes.

gold_claim: Data-efficiency trends optimized on IID validation loss reportedly transfer to downstream benchmarks.
supporting_text: even though we're only chasing IID VAT loss in all of our experiments, pretty much all of the trends in this paper directly work on downstream benchmarks.
why_valuable: Connects an in-distribution optimization result to broader downstream utility.
verification_requirement: verify IID validation terminology and benchmark results
